chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI C.P. No.D-2123 of 2013 Telenor LDI Communications (Pvt.) Ltd. Versus Federation of Pakistan & others A N D C.P. D-6847 of 2018 Telecard Ltd. Versus Competition Appellate Tribunal & another Date Order with signature of Judge Dated: 20.09.2024 Mr. Ijaz Ahmed for petitioner in C.P. No.D-2123 of 2013 Mr. Zarrar Ahmed holds brief for Mr. Sarmad Hani for petitioner in C.P. No.D-6847 of 2018. Mr. Khaleeq Ahmed, Deputy Attorney General. Mr. Khurram Rasheed for CCP/respondent No.2 in C.P. No.D-2123 of 2013. Mr. Mohammad Ali Ghanghro for CCP/respondent No.1 in C.P. No.D-6847 of 2018. Mr. Ali Akbar Sahito, Deputy Director (Law) P.T.A. -.-.- These are the two petitions which were left out from the bunch of petitions of similar nature on the count that the instructions were not passed on to Mr. Ijaz Ahmed, learned counsel for petitioner appearing in C.P. No.D-2123 of 2013, and the counsel appearing in the other petition. Today Mr. Ijaz Ahmed and the Mr. Zarrar Ahmed holding brief for Mr. Sarmad Hani in the connected petition submit that these petitions may also be disposed of on the same terms. However, we have been informed by Mr. Khurram Rasheed that petitioner in C.P. No.D-2123 of 2013 has already surrendered before the concerned Tribunal wherein interim orders are already operating hence the injunctive order of the nature as was granted earlier to the petitioners in the bunch of petitions, which were disposed of on 12.09.2024, is essentially not required. In case the Tribunal has already granted the interim injunction, perhaps on this count, Mr. Khurram Rasheed should not have any objection for disposal of these petitions on the same terms as such injunction orders are already secured/granted by the Tribunal subject to final outcome.

In case the Tribunal has already granted the interim injunction, perhaps on this count, Mr. Khurram Rasheed should not have any objection for disposal of these petitions on the same terms as such injunction orders are already secured/granted by the Tribunal subject to final outcome. In view above these two petitions are also disposed of with the observation that the petitioners are at liberty to address and plead their case before the Tribunal and for the additional grounds, if any, they may seek permission from the Tribunal. It is further observed that till such appeals are decided by the Tribunal no adverse action be taken against the petitioners. Both the petitions stands disposed of in the above terms along with pending applications. Chief Justice Judge
